SVG: clean ruled data table with header 'Hormone | Role' listing Estrogen, FSH, LH and Progesterone with their bulleted roles in the menstrual cycle.

SVG: line graph of hormone levels during the menstrual cycle. X-axis Day 0-28 (ticks 0,7,14,21,28); y-axis qualitative hormone levels (no numeric ticks). Four distinct unlabelled curves (key blank for student): FSH small bump peaking ~day 12; LH sharp narrow spike at ovulation day 14; estrogen peak ~day 12-13 then dip with a second broad peak ~day 21; progesterone broad peak ~day 21. Ovulation marked at day 14.

SVG: line graph of percentage of maximum activity vs pH for five digestive enzymes. X-axis pH 0-11 (integer ticks); y-axis % of maximum activity 0,25,50,75,100. Five bell curves: pepsin optimum pH 2 (~28% at pH 0), salivary amylase optimum pH 6, carbonic anhydrase optimum pH 7, trypsin optimum pH 8, alkaline phosphatase optimum pH 9 (peak ~77%). Key labels all five.
